title: Smoky Italian Fennel Sausage Casserole
description: A hearty, cloudfired casserole that blends Italian fennel sausage with Malaysian spices, baked to a silky finish that feels like a cozy tandoor treat.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat and brown the sausage slices until caramelized, about 3–4 minutes. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining olive oil, then sauté the onion and bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste, coating the vegetables, and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in tomato sauce and coconut milk, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Return the browned sausage to the skillet, stir to combine, and season with salt and pepper. Let the mixture cook for 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Transfer the entire mixture to a baking dish, creating an even layer.

Bake uncovered for 20 minutes, then reduce the heat to 160 °C (320 °F) and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is slightly crisp and the sauce has thickened. (Internal temperature of sausage should reach 70 °C /158 °F.)
Remove from oven, sprinkle chopped coriander over the top, and let rest 5 minutes before serving.
